How do I handle a coworker who takes credit for my work?

Well, honey, that's a real pickle, isn't it? It's understandable that you'd feel frustrated. Nobody likes to see their hard work go unnoticed, especially when someone else is reaping the rewards.

Now, I'm no expert on your workplace dynamics, and you probably know your coworker and the office culture better than I ever could. But I've learned a thing or two about human nature over the years. 

Sometimes, people take credit for things they haven't done simply because they're insecure. Maybe they're feeling threatened by your talent, or maybe they just crave that little bit of validation.  

Before you confront them directly, maybe try a gentler approach. Casually mention your contribution in a team meeting, highlighting your specific role in the project. You could say something like, "I was so glad we were able to incorporate my research on [topic] into the final presentation. It really helped us achieve [positive outcome]."

If that doesn't work, you might need to have a private conversation with your coworker. But remember, honey, go in with empathy. Start by assuming the best of them. Maybe they genuinely forgot your contribution.

Say something like, "Hey, I noticed you mentioned [project] in the meeting. I just wanted to clarify that I was heavily involved in [specific task]. I'm sure you didn't mean to leave me out, but I want to make sure everyone understands the full scope of the work."

Remember, your goal isn't to start a fight, it's to protect your own interests and ensure you get the recognition you deserve. Be confident, be clear, and be kind. And if all else fails, well, sometimes you just have to let it go and focus on your own success. After all, your brilliance will shine through eventually.
